content: ICM curiosity-driven exploration can be reengineered to actively prune low-value information from an agent's working memory to offset inherent working memory capacity limits that bottleneck reasoning. Curiosity, typically framed only as a driver for discovering new environmental information, can double as a natural filter that retains only high-uncertainty, high-prediction-error information that directly advances reasoning, dropping inert low-relevance data that would otherwise clog limited working memory.
source_topics: agent working memory capacity limits reasoning
trigger_topic: agent working memory capacity limits reasoning
